methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
INERATEC is a Series B climate and energy technology company that has raised $129M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of nine comparable companies—one public (LNZA) and eight private firms operating in the same sector and stage. The private peer set includes Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), among others, with valuations ranging from $120M to $5.0B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the company's $129M total raised as a proxy base and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $245M. The peer-median method benchmarks INERATEC against the six comparable companies at the same stage, producing a median valuation of $355M. The peer cohort exhibits substantial dispersion, with the median driven significantly higher by outlier valuations (Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B).

These methods produce a triangulated valuation range of $208M (low) to $408M (high), with a median of $300M. The wide peer dispersion and the outsized influence of high-valuation outliers in a small sample represent the primary uncertainty. The stage-progression method, anchored to capital raised, supplies a more conservative floor, while peer-median reflects current market pricing of comparable stage assets in the sector.
